108.status: Started bothering him after his March 15 outing, and the Angels are going to be cautious with him. Played catch on March 26 and said his knee has been improving.
Hitting off a tee and taking ground balls back at the club's Spring Training complex in Arizona and isn't expected to be out long.
Has been playing catch and doing strengthening exercises and he's feeling good. Has yet to throw a bullpen since the injury but played catch in Houston on March 26.
Nail on his right middle finger bothered him in his March 17 start and hasn't improved much. Working through mechanical issues at the club's Spring Training complex in Arizona.
Progressing well in his throwing program. Angels manager Kurt Suzuki said he looks really good and wouldn't be surprised if he comes back sooner than expected.
Scheduled to be evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas. “There is a concern for the state of my UCL right now,” said Stephenson, who now hopes to pitch this year.” Surgery is a possibility.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Felt discomfort while warming up in bullpen on March 21. Underwent imaging in Baltimore on March 23. Placed on IL to begin season.
Sustained injury during March 19 Grapefruit League game. Underwent imaging in Baltimore on March 23. Placed on IL to begin season.
Threw multiple bullpen sessions late in Spring Training. Close to progressing to live batting practice, as of March 21. Will need Minor League rehab assignment.
Underwent procedure to remove bone from right hand on Feb. 12. Took live batting practice March 19 and 20. Will begin rehab assignment in Triple-A Norfolk's opener on March 27.
After testing, received platelet-rich plasma injection on Feb. 20. Has been running, fielding ground balls and swinging bat with left hand as of March 16.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.
Placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 14. No clear timeline for potential return yet.

111.status: Recently cleared a key hurdle when he was given clearance to run the bases and slide. Will stay at extended spring training until ready to go on Minors rehab assignment.
Should soon be ready for Minors rehab assignment.

111.expectedReturn: Will miss significant time at start of season
111.status: Underwent left shoulder arthroscopic debridement surgery on March 12 and was placed on 60-day injured list. Had a PRP injection in early February, and has not participated in any baseball activity.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Started gradual throwing program on flat ground in the second week of Spring Training.

112.status: Injured March 14 during Classic. Has resumed hitting, throwing and light running (with modified knee brace). Will continue rehab in Chicago during first homestand.
Resumed playing catch on March 3 after 10-day shutdown. Moved back to mound March 16. Could return to games by mid-April.
Shut down from throwing on March 8. Could be cleared to resume playing catch around April 1, per manager Craig Counsell.

Medically cleared Feb. 28 by Dr. Keith Meister, who performed Steele's elbow surgery (left ulnar collateral ligament revision repair) on April 18, 2025. Has advanced to facing hitters in live BP sessions.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Cubs manager Craig Counsell said Austin could miss "months" while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

113.status: Ferguson's second MRI in Cincinnati on March 25 went well and confirmed he's healing well. Cleared to begin a throwing program. Will travel with the club on the road.
Lodolo took part in a light throwing session on March 24. He exited his final Cactus League start on March 22 after 10 pitches due to a blister.
Had arthroscopic surgery March 11 to remove bone chips and loose bodies from elbow. Shut down from throwing for six weeks; expected to miss 14-16 weeks.

114.status: Exited his Opening Day start on March 26 after warming up ahead of the bottom of the 6th inning. Was feeling better postgame and did well on strength tests. Team will see how he feels in coming days.
Threw in second Minor League game on March 24. Could begin rehab assignment with Triple-A Columbus week of March 30.
Played in Minor League game on March 23 as build-up continues. Team hopeful for short IL stint.
Threw live batting practice on March 18. Has been throwing bullpen sessions and from 120-150 feet on flat ground.
Day to day after X-rays came back negative for a fracture.
Suffered moderate grade strain during Feb. 24 appearance against the Dodgers that is expected to sideline him for 6-8 weeks.

115.status: Will start the season on the injured list for an undetermined amount of time, although skipper Warren Schaeffer says it doesn’t figure to be a long stint.
Dr. Keith Meister performed Tommy John surgery with an internal brace March 20 in Dallas. Petit, a Rule 5 Draft pick from the Tigers, will miss the season.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: In late March, he was throwing bullpens, with an eye toward facing hitters as the season starts.

115.expectedReturn: TBD, owing to the trickiness of such injuries
115.status: Late in Spring Training, Crim noted that he has never missed time with injury, so he is deferring to the training staff and not speculating when he will be available.

115.expectedReturn: Most likely headed to the Minors when cleared
115.status: Injured his knee while sliding trying to make a catch in the outfield.
Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
Targeting the middle of April to return to Minor League action.
There is no return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Placed on 15-day IL on March 25, retroactive to March 22. Has thrown three batting practice sessions. Completing rehab daily.
Placed on 10-day IL on March 25, retroactive to March 22. Completing a return-to-play throwing program that began on March 12. Facing live pitching. Completing defensive drills.
Threw a bullpen session on March 24.
Suffered a groin strain while doing a routine workout off the mound. Placed on 60-day IL on March 23. Completing rehab daily.
Playing catch out to 120 feet three times the week of March 23. Underwent Tommy John surgery on June 16.
Completing a return-to-play throwing program that began on March 9. Placed on 60-day IL on March 10.

Underwent labrum repair surgery on right shoulder on Feb. 2, performed by Dr. Keith Meister, after offseason rehab program was unsuccessful. Expected to miss 2026 season but should be ready for Spring Training in 2027.

117.status: Hader, who was shut down in early February, is throwing bullpens and should begin facing hitters in mid-April, according to GM Dana Brown.
Sprained his knee early in spring camp, but was pitching in Grapefruit League games toward the end of Spring Training.
Strained his oblique in a March 12 Grapefruit League game and expected to be out for a while.
At the end of spring, he was throwing in the bullpen after experiencing elbow soreness midway through camp. He had offseason elbow surgery.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Underwent surgery to repair ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace as well as a repair of the flexor tendon in his forearm on June 6, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow May 23, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Suffered Grade 1+ oblique strain on Feb. 25 and was able to begin bullpens mid-March. Will continue ramp-up in Arizona before rehab assignment.
Suffered calf strain on March 6 and left off Opening Day roster, but could join team during opening homestand if recovery stays on track.
Missed all of 2025 after right elbow surgery, so took ramp-up this spring slow. Expected to continue it in Arizona before a rehab assignment.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Underwent shoulder debridement procedure in September 2025. He's set to begin facing live hitters on March 27 and could start an extended rehab assignment not long after.
In a "holding pattern," per general manager Brandon Gomes, as the team waits for his side tightness to subside before he can resume a throwing program.
Dealt with shoulder soreness during Spring Training. He progressed to throwing off the mound by the time the Dodgers broke camp, but the team is building him up slowly.
Slow-played his offseason throwing program due to lingering discomfort. He has resumed throwing bullpen sessions at a light intensity level.
Imaging revealed inflammation, but not structural damage, in Stone's shoulder. He's pain-free and could begin a throwing program soon.
Velocity is down from where it is expected to be, so the Dodgers are slow-playing him. He is ramping up to greater distances in his catch play.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025. He started throwing full bullpen sessions in mid-March.

120.status: Felt soreness pitching on March 20, has been shut down from throwing activity. Nationals are being cautious because Gray is returning from Tommy John surgery.
Schultz did not appear in a Spring Training game after Feb. 28.
Progressing through throwing program; threw a bullpen session on March 20.
Continuing throwing progression; throwing on flat ground at 125 feet.

120.playerName: Jarlin Susana (Nationals' No. 4 prospect, MLB No. 80)
120.status: Threw 15 pitches at a distance of 60 feet on Feb. 20; continuing throwing progressing, with flat ground throwing three times a week.

120.playerName: Travis Sykora (Nationals' No. 2 prospect, MLB No. 54)
120.status: Continuing throwing progression, including throwing on flat ground three times a week.

121.status: Is about a month behind other Mets pitchers. Will spend early April in Port St. Lucie rehabbing and pitching in camp games before advancing to an affiliate.
Exited the Mets' March 21 game after four innings due to knee discomfort and underwent an MRI, which revealed the tear. Will spend his recovery on the Minor League IL.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Will need 4-8 weeks to recover, after which he can commence his buildup.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

134.status: After "de-load" week, back to throwing sides and is expected to throw live batting practice early the week of March 23.

135.status: Underwent quad surgery in September. Returned in the Cactus League, making two scoreless appearances, but wasn't built up enough for Opening Day. Scheduled to begin a rehab stint in late March.
Missed the 2025 season but hoped to return early in '26. Made one spring start but experienced soreness afterward and was sidelined. Has resumed playing catch but has yet to throw off a mound.

Sustained an oblique injury during the offseason, then aggravated it during a game on March 5. Returned on March 22 in a Triple-A game but will open the season on the IL and is expected to need a rehab assignment.
Faced the Mariners in a "B" game on a back field on March 20, his first game-like setting since his 2025 season ended in June because of the injury. Pitched a scoreless inning with three strikeouts.
Missed the World Baseball Classic due to the injury, then began the season on the IL, but could face hitters in late March.
Not built up enough to make the Opening Day roster. Resumed throwing bullpens in early March and pitched in two Cactus League games in late March.
Only appeared in five Cactus League games after sustaining the injury in early March. By the end of camp, had begun doing light drills.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery as well as a flexor tendon repair operation in April 2025. Has resumed throwing bullpens in Padres camp.

136.status: Still hadn't begun full catch play by March 22, but took swings in the cage for the third straight day on March 21, upon returning from a consultation with Dr. Keith Meister, which included a cortisone shot.

Threw 30-pitch bullpen session with no issues on March 17. Manager Dan Wilson said Miller will throw “maybe another couple bullpen sessions where he’s not feeling it, and we’ll kind of go from there.”
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.

137.status: Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.Read more >>
Has been throwing bullpen sessions but is still working his way back from his September 2024 left shoulder surgery.
Continuing his rehab progression and is currently throwing out to 90 feet.
Suffered a high grade strain of his right hip flexor while pitching for Team Colombia on March 3 and is expected to miss up to three months.
Underwent surgery to repair the anterior capsule in his right shoulder in May 2025. Started a bullpen progression on Feb. 10 and is throwing twice per week.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025. Will begin a throwing progression in mid-March.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Underwent surgery last October to address Haglund’s deformities in both heels. Started his running progression on March 20, but no date has been set for a return to the lineup.
Dobbins will start the season on the injured list. He is able to pitch but is being held out of agility drills. The team is still mapping out the next steps of Dobbins' rehab.

139.status: Visited Dr. Koco Eaton on March 23 after feeling "OK" in last game. Traveled with team to St. Louis, and he will make 15 throws from second base per day in pregame work.

Threw a bullpen on March 23 and received an injection on March 25. Will be shut down for only two days before resuming throwing. "Expected to be a short absence," per baseball operations president Erik Neander.

“Didn’t feel great” when playing catch Feb. 19. Received a cortisone injection on Feb. 20. After bullpens, scheduled for live BPs on March 27 and 31. Triple-A rehab assignment should begin soon after.

139.injury: Rib tip syndrome, right oblique strain
139.status: Felt something in his ribs/ab/oblique after hitting on March 17. Underwent imaging on March 18. Was told he'll miss a minimum of 3-4 weeks.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Will resume throwing around April 6, but will need a while to build up after being shut down so long.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July. Placed on 60-day IL on Feb. 10.

140.status: Threw 25 pitches during live batting practice at Globe Life Field on March 23. Manager Skip Schumaker called it an “exciting” development, noting Bradford looked in control of his pitches.
Took a comebacker off the bat of Kyle Higashioka in batting practice and will be out for about four weeks.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.
Continuing to rehab a back injury that kept him sidelined for much of 2025. Behind in his throwing progression, but should return by the start of the Triple-A season.

141.status: Continues to throw in Minor League games in Florida as he ramps up from the issue experienced earlier in Spring Training.
Will join the Blue Jays in Toronto for the Opening Day ceremonies, then continue to rehab at the club's complex in Florida. He has not thrown off a mound yet.
Garcia is throwing off a mound, so he will continue to ramp up and could be a factor in the Blue Jays' bullpen early in the season.
Initial belief is that Berríos could pitch through this injury, but a timeline has not yet been set.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

Was scheduled to begin throwing again March 10, which is a step in the right direction after being shut down the past couple of weeks. The Blue Jays were working to find some preventative measures to help Tiedemann avoid further elbow soreness in the future.

142.status: Has begun throwing but is expected to begin season on injured list.
Has begun playing catch; threw at 60 feet on March 19 and 75 feet on March 20.

142.injury: “Significant tearing” in UCL in right elbow
142.status: Underwent surgery on Feb. 25 to repair, rather than reconstruct, the UCL and will miss the season.More >

143.status: He will begin a rehab assignment on March 28 with Triple-A Lehigh Valley. Pitched in a Minor League game on March 23 in Clearwater.
He will begin a rehab assignment on March 28 with Triple-A Lehigh Valley. He might need only four or five more rehab starts before he is ready to rejoin the rotation.

143.playerName: Aidan Miller (Phillies' No. 1 prospect, No. 23 overall)
143.status: There is no timetable for his return to baseball activity. “We don't want to push him too fast and then all of a sudden, we go backwards,” Dave Dombrowski said.
Did not play this spring before being optioned to Minor League camp on March 7. Has been swinging at bat as of March 22.
Left Team Israel in the World Baseball Classic after feeling something in his abdomen/oblique. Phillies manager Rob Thomson said it’s “very mild.”

144.status: Felt some discomfort after his March 17 start in a Minor League game. Early reports regarding his progress have been encouraging. He may end up missing just a start or two. He’ll be eligible for activation on April 6.

144.injury: Torn tendon in right middle finger
144.status: Arrived at camp March 16. Hasn't yet been cleared to swing a bat, but will steadily increase baseball activities. Kim was injured when he slipped on a sheet of ice in January.

Began taking batting practice on March 9 and was participating in the team’s defensive drills by the end of camp. The Braves are hoping the 2023 All-Star catcher returns from hip surgery during the first half of May.
Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery on Feb. 23 to have loose bodies removed from his right elbow. Could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Underwent surgery on his right elbow on June 9.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. There is a chance he could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

145.status: Exited March 14 start with right elbow soreness. Will undergo season-ending Tommy John surgery in the coming weeks; the procedure will be performed by Dr. Keith Meister.
Placed on 60-day IL as he continues to recover from Tommy John surgery he underwent last year. Has thrown numerous times off the mound.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.
Has been off the mound a few times and is feeling good in his recovery from Tommy John surgery he underwent March 7, 2025.
Continues with the bullpen portion of his recovery ramp-up, having thrown seven. Has felt great as the rehab process moves forward.
Hopes to return sometime after the April 2 home opener, but no official timetable. He’s with the team in Milwaukee
Hasn't played in a game since March 6 during a Cactus League contest. General manager Chris Getz said they will find out something soon in regard to Baldwin’s next move.

146.status: Reassigned to Minor League camp on March 3. Felt discomfort during his Grapefruit League debut on Feb. 26.
Underwent core muscle surgery on Feb. 27. Sustained left groin injury during offseason workouts. Rested before arriving in Jupiter, where it flared up again.

Performing baseball activities. Placed on 10-day injured list on March 25 (retroactive to March 22). Will be reassessed in 3-4 weeks from March 22. Felt tightness on March 20 rounding third base. Missed 1-2 weeks with a strain of the same hamstring earlier this spring.
Placed on 10-day injured list on March 25 (retroactive to March 22). 6-8 week timeline from March 22. Replaced during third-inning at-bat after fouling off an 89.6 mph cutter.

Placed on 10-day injured list on March 25 (retroactive to March 22). Imaging revealed severity after being scratched from March 11 lineup. Stretched his side making a backhanded play during individual pregame work that day.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Placed on 60-day injured list on March 25. Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Placed on 60-day IL on Feb. 12. Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.status: Made two Grapefruit League starts and will continue to pitch in game situations, including Minor League games, over the next several weeks. Underwent Tommy John surgery in March 2025.

Threw 35 pitches in live batting practice on March 19, his second time facing live hitters since October elbow surgery to remove loose bodies and shave a bone spur. Remained behind in Tampa to continue rehab when team began regular season.

Is hitting overhand tosses and could advance to live at-bats on or around April 1. Volpe is participating in all defensive and baserunning drills and began diving on his left shoulder in early March. Underwent arthroscopic surgery Oct. 12.
Spent most of Spring Training throwing on flat ground. Tentatively scheduled to begin throwing bullpen sessions in late March. Could face hitters in April or May.
Recovering from February 2025 procedure. Has thrown eight bullpens as of mid-February. Said Feb. 14 he is not sure when game action will begin. "I'm taking it slow right now."

158.status: Aggravated a previous injury on a check swing during exhibition games against the Reds, and an MRI scan revealed a small fracture. (last updated: March 26)

158.expectedReturn: No sooner than late April/early May
158.status: Estimated to begin a rehab assignment in "early April."
Diagnosed with a left rhomboid strain while pitching in the World Baseball Classic and will open the season on rehab assignment with Triple-A Nashville.
Suffered right soleus injury on March 12. Aiming for April 15 return to games.
Has been DHing in games and progressing to outfield play.
Injured running to first base on March 9, and an MRI revealed a more serious injury than initially believed.
A non-roster invitee to big league camp, Garabito had a bone fragment removed and a tendon repaired in his foot. He's expected to miss about four months.
A former top prospect who was in camp as a non-roster invitee, Garcia was injured playing winter ball.
